target.title: fix(whatsappdb): clamp impossible Apple-epoch timestamps so --json survives 0@status
target.body:
## Problem

`wacrawl --json chats` / `unread` / `status` fail with:

```
json: error calling MarshalJSON for type time.Time: Time.MarshalJSON: year outside of range [0,9999]
```

and emit empty stdout + exit 1. The human (table) output is unaffected, so it only surfaces under `--json`.

## Cause

WhatsApp Desktop stores a sentinel `ZLASTMESSAGEDATE` for pseudo-chats such as the `0@status` broadcast chat. `appleTime()` interprets it as a literal Apple-epoch value, which converts to an impossible year (~11001). That value lands in the archive, and `encoding/json` then can't marshal it (`time.Time.MarshalJSON` rejects years outside `[0,9999]`) for any command whose JSON output includes the row — `chats`/`unread` directly, `status` via `max(ts)`. It also makes the pseudo-chat sort to the top of chat listings.

## Fix

Normalize impossible Apple-epoch conversions to the zero value in `appleNullTime` — the single function every imported timestamp flows through — so the sentinel never enters the archive (and sorts as oldest rather than newest). One-line, root-cause fix; no public JSON model types change.

## Test

`TestAppleNullTimeNormalizesSentinel` covers: a sentinel converts to zero, a valid timestamp still converts correctly, and invalid/non-positive input stays zero (unchanged behaviour). `go test ./...` passes.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)


---

## Real-behavior proof (redacted)

macOS, real WhatsApp Desktop archive. Both sides built from source. `--sync never` reads the stored snapshot deterministically (no re-import); `--sync always` forces a fresh import. Private paths/content redacted.

```console
# 1) Reproduce on origin/main @ 7c4f382 (import re-stores the 0@status sentinel):
$ wacrawl --sync never --json chats ; echo "exit=$?"
json: error calling MarshalJSON for type time.Time: Time.MarshalJSON: year outside of range [0,9999]
exit=1
$ wacrawl --sync never --json unread ; echo "exit=$?"
json: error calling MarshalJSON for type time.Time: Time.MarshalJSON: year outside of range [0,9999]
exit=1

#    stored sentinel row (year ~11001):
$ sqlite3 ~/.wacrawl/wacrawl.db 'select count(*),sum(last_message_at>253402300799),max(last_message_at) from chats'
176 chats | 1 out-of-range | max=284990875200

# 2) Fix branch @ 2dcd2ff — one re-import clamps the sentinel; JSON now succeeds:
$ wacrawl --sync always --json status | jq -e . >/dev/null  # OK ; echo "exit=$?"
exit=0
$ wacrawl --sync always --json chats | jq 'length'  # OK, 50 records ; echo "exit=$?"
exit=0
$ wacrawl --sync always --json unread | jq 'length'  # OK, 26 records ; echo "exit=$?"
exit=0

#    stored sentinel row cleared after re-import:
$ sqlite3 ~/.wacrawl/wacrawl.db 'select count(*),sum(last_message_at>253402300799),max(last_message_at) from chats'
176 chats | 0 out-of-range | max=1780911191 (2026-06-08)
```

### Note for already-populated archives

The clamp happens at **import** (`appleNullTime`), so an archive already populated by a pre-fix build keeps the stored sentinel until its next import. Because import is a full delete + re-insert, any single `wacrawl sync` (or the next `--sync auto` after the staleness window) flushes it — see step 2 above. Fresh archives are never affected.
